We differentiate f(x) = (-x - 6)/(2·x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 1/(2·x^2).
Answer: 1/(2·x^2)

We differentiate f(x) = (6·x - 3)/(3·x - 6) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 1/(3·(x - 2)^2).
Answer: 1/(3·(x - 2)^2)

We differentiate f(x) = 6·x^2·sin(x) - 4·x·sin(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 6·x^2·cos(x) + 12·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x - pi/4) - 4·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4).
Answer: 6·x^2·cos(x) + 12·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x - pi/4) - 4·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4)

We differentiate f(x) = cos(-2·x^2 + x + 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-4·x + 1)·sin(-2·x^2 + x + 1).
Answer: (-4·x + 1)·sin(-2·x^2 + x + 1)

We differentiate f(x) = sin(2·x^2 + 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 4·x·cos(2·x^2 + 1).
Answer: 4·x·cos(2·x^2 + 1)

We differentiate f(x) = 3·x^2·sin(x) - 5·x·sin(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 3·x^2·cos(x) + 6·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x - pi/4) - 5·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4).
Answer: 3·x^2·cos(x) + 6·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x - pi/4) - 5·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4)

We differentiate f(x) = (2·x - 2)/(-4·x - 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 2/(4·x + 1)^2.
Answer: 2/(4·x + 1)^2

We differentiate f(x) = -3·x/(-5·x - 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 15/(5·x + 3)^2.
Answer: 15/(5·x + 3)^2

We differentiate f(x) = (5·x + 5)/(6·x - 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 10/(6·x - 1)^2.
Answer: 10/(6·x - 1)^2

We differentiate f(x) = e^(-x^2 - 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = -2·x·e^(-x^2 - 3).
Answer: -2·x·e^(-x^2 - 3)

We differentiate f(x) = (3 - 3·x)/(-5·x - 5) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 3/(5·(x + 1)^2).
Answer: 3/(5·(x + 1)^2)

We differentiate f(x) = -2·x·e^(x)·cos(x) - 2·e^(x)·cos(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = -2·(sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4) + sin(x))·e^(x).
Answer: -2·(sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4) + sin(x))·e^(x)

We differentiate f(x) = 3·x^2·e^(x) - 2·x·e^(x) - 5·e^(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 3·(x^2 + x - 1)·e^(x).
Answer: 3·(x^2 + x - 1)·e^(x)

We differentiate f(x) = (5·x + 6)/(5 - 3·x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = -19/(3·x - 5)^2.
Answer: -19/(3·x - 5)^2

We differentiate f(x) = e^(2·x^2 - 3·x + 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 2·(4·x - 3)·e^(2·x^2 - 3·x + 1).
Answer: 2·(4·x - 3)·e^(2·x^2 - 3·x + 1)

We differentiate f(x) = e^(x^2 + x - 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (2·x + 1)·e^(x^2 + x - 3).
Answer: (2·x + 1)·e^(x^2 + x - 3)

We differentiate f(x) = (-4·x^2 - 6·x)/(2 - 4·x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 2·(-4·x^2 + 8·x - 3)/(16·x^2 - 4·x + 1).
Answer: 2·(-4·x^2 + 8·x - 3)/(16·x^2 - 4·x + 1)

We differentiate f(x) = -sin(3·x^2 - 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = -6·x·cos(3·x^2 - 1).
Answer: -6·x·cos(3·x^2 - 1)

We differentiate f(x) = 2·x·sin(x) - 5·sin(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 2·x·cos(x) + 2·sin(x) - 5·sqrt(2)·sin(x + pi/4).
Answer: 2·x·cos(x) + 2·sin(x) - 5·sqrt(2)·sin(x + pi/4)

We differentiate f(x) = e^(2·x^2 - 3·x + 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 2·(4·x - 3)·e^(2·x^2 - 3·x + 3).
Answer: 2·(4·x - 3)·e^(2·x^2 - 3·x + 3)

We differentiate f(x) = -sin(3·x^2 + 3·x - 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-6·x - 3)·cos(3·x^2 + 3·x - 3).
Answer: (-6·x - 3)·cos(3·x^2 + 3·x - 3)

We differentiate f(x) = (x^2 - x - 3)/(3 - x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-x^2 + 6·x - 10)/(x^2 - 6·x + 9).
Answer: (-x^2 + 6·x - 10)/(x^2 - 6·x + 9)

We differentiate f(x) = (x - 1)/(4 - 5·x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 1/(5·(x - 2)^2).
Answer: 1/(5·(x - 2)^2)

We differentiate f(x) = 6·x^2·cos(x) + x·cos(x) + 2·cos(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = -6·sqrt(2)·x^2·sin(x + pi/4) - x·sin(x) - 2·x·cos(x) + cos(x).
Answer: -6·sqrt(2)·x^2·sin(x + pi/4) - x·sin(x) - 2·x·cos(x) + cos(x)

We differentiate f(x) = -5·x^2·e^(x) - 3·x·e^(x) + e^(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-5·x^2 - 13·x + 4)·e^(x).
Answer: (-5·x^2 - 13·x + 4)·e^(x)

We differentiate f(x) = (3·x + 2)/(x + 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 7/(x + 3)^2.
Answer: 7/(x + 3)^2

We differentiate f(x) = e^(-2·x^2 - 3·x + 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-4·x - 3)·e^(-2·x^2 - 3·x + 3).
Answer: (-4·x - 3)·e^(-2·x^2 - 3·x + 3)

We differentiate f(x) = (2·x - 1)^2 with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 8·x - 4.
Answer: 8·x - 4

We differentiate f(x) = (-3·x^2 - 2·x - 6)/(4·x - 2) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-3·x^2 + 6·x - 1)/(4·x^2 - 2·x + 1).
Answer: (-3·x^2 + 6·x - 1)/(4·x^2 - 2·x + 1)

We differentiate f(x) = cos(2·x^2 + 3·x + 2) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-4·x - 3)·sin(2·x^2 + 3·x + 2).
Answer: (-4·x - 3)·sin(2·x^2 + 3·x + 2)

We differentiate f(x) = -4·x^2·sin(x) + 4·x·sin(x) - 2·sin(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = -4·x^2·cos(x) - 8·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x + pi/4) + 4·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4).
Answer: -4·x^2·cos(x) - 8·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x + pi/4) + 4·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4)

We differentiate f(x) = sin(x^2 + 2·x + 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (2·x + 2)·cos(x^2 + 2·x + 3).
Answer: (2·x + 2)·cos(x^2 + 2·x + 3)

We differentiate f(x) = 2·x^2·sin(x) - 5·x·sin(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 2·x^2·cos(x) + 4·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x - pi/4) - 5·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4).
Answer: 2·x^2·cos(x) + 4·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x - pi/4) - 5·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4)

We differentiate f(x) = -3·x^2·cos(x) + 6·x·cos(x) - 5·cos(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 3·x^2·sin(x) - 6·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4) + sin(x).
Answer: 3·x^2·sin(x) - 6·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4) + sin(x)

We differentiate f(x) = 5·x^2·sin(x) + 5·x·sin(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 5·x^2·cos(x) + 10·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x + pi/4).
Answer: 5·x^2·cos(x) + 10·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x + pi/4)

We differentiate f(x) = e^(3·x^2 - x - 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (6·x - 1)·e^(3·x^2 - x - 1).
Answer: (6·x - 1)·e^(3·x^2 - x - 1)

We differentiate f(x) = -6·x·sin(x)·cos(x) - 5·sin(x)·cos(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = -6·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4) - 5·sqrt(2)·sin(x + pi/4).
Answer: -6·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4) - 5·sqrt(2)·sin(x + pi/4)

We differentiate f(x) = 6·x^2·e^(x) - x·e^(x) - e^(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 6·(x^2 + x - 1)·e^(x).
Answer: 6·(x^2 + x - 1)·e^(x)

We differentiate f(x) = -x^2·sin(x) - 3·x·sin(x) + 6·sin(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = -x^2·cos(x) - 2·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x + pi/4) - 3·x·cos(x) - sin(x).
Answer: -x^2·cos(x) - 2·sqrt(2)·x·sin(x + pi/4) - 3·x·cos(x) - sin(x)

We differentiate f(x) = (x - 4)/(3·x + 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 13/(3·x + 1)^2.
Answer: 13/(3·x + 1)^2

We differentiate f(x) = e^(4·x + 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 4·e^(4·x + 3).
Answer: 4·e^(4·x + 3)

We differentiate f(x) = -5·x·e^(x)·cos(x) - 6·e^(x)·cos(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-5·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4) - 6·sin(x) - cos(x))·e^(x).
Answer: (-5·sqrt(2)·x·cos(x + pi/4) - 6·sin(x) - cos(x))·e^(x)

We integrate f(x) = 4·e^(2·x) using reversing the derivative of the exponential. The antiderivative is F(x) = 2·e^(2·x) + C, which can be checked since F'(x) = f(x).
Answer: 2·e^(2·x)

We differentiate f(x) = e^(2·x^2 - 2·x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 2·(2·x - 1)·e^(x·(2·x - 1)).
Answer: 2·(2·x - 1)·e^(x·(2·x - 1))

We integrate f(x) = (5·x - 1)^3 using a linear/substitution recognition. The antiderivative is F(x) = (5·x - 1)^4/20 + C, which can be checked since F'(x) = f(x).
Answer: (5·x - 1)^4/20

We differentiate f(x) = -2·x·e^(x) - 6·e^(x) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-2·sqrt(2) - 6)·e^(x).
Answer: (-2·sqrt(2) - 6)·e^(x)

We differentiate f(x) = cos(3·x^2 - 2·x + 3) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (2 - 6·x)·sin(3·x^2 - 2·x + 3).
Answer: (2 - 6·x)·sin(3·x^2 - 2·x + 3)

We differentiate f(x) = sin(x^2 - 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = 2·x·cos(x^2 - 1).
Answer: 2·x·cos(x^2 - 1)

We differentiate f(x) = sin(x^2 + 2·x + 1) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (2·x + 2)·cos(x^2 + 2·x + 1).
Answer: (2·x + 2)·cos(x^2 + 2·x + 1)

We differentiate f(x) = (-4·x^2 + 2·x + 3)/(6·x + 2) with respect to x, applying the standard rules (power/product/quotient/chain as needed). This gives f'(x) = (-2·x^2 - 4·x + 1)/(3·(9·x^2 + 6·x + 4)).
Answer: (-2·x^2 - 4·x + 1)/(3·(9·x^2 + 6·x + 4))
